content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 29, 2017

Katharine L. Elliott
Mendocino County Counsel
Office of The County Counsel
Administration Center
501 Low Gap Road, RM. 1030
Ukiah, CA 95482

Re:    Your Request for Advice
       Our File No. W-17-183

Dear Ms. Elliott:

       This letter addresses your request for formal written advice on behalf of Mendocino County
Supervisor Dan Hamburg regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹
Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) authorizes the Commission to decline to provide formal written advice
if the requester is seeking advice relating to past conduct. Your request involves analysis of
governmental decisions that have already occurred. We provided informal advice prior to those
decisions. Because the Board of Supervisors has already addressed those decisions, your request for
formal written advice relates to past conduct. Therefore, we decline to provide formal written
advice at this time pursuant to Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).

       If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

                                                            Sincerely,

                                                            Jack C. Woodside
                                                            General Counsel

                                                            [signature]

       By:    Matthew F. Christy
              Counsel, Legal Division

MFC:jgl


¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, Sections 18109-18996,
of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.conclusion: Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) authorizes the Commission to decline to provide formal written advice if the requester is seeking advice relating to past conduct. Your request involves analysis of governmental decisions that have already occurred. We provided informal advice prior to those decisions. Because the Board of Supervisors has already addressed those decisions, your request for formal written advice relates to past conduct. Therefore, we decline to provide formal written advice at this time pursuant to Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Mendocino County Supervisor Dan Hamburg violated conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act regarding past governmental decisions made by the Board of Supervisors.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide formal written advice because the request relates to past governmental conduct that has already been addressed by the Board of Supervisors, and informal advice was already provided prior to those decisions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with no formal Question or Conclusion sections but clear legal reasoning provided. File number W-17-183 indicates withdrawal/declination type. Informal advice was previously provided.

Your request involves analysis of governmental decisions that have already occurred. We provided informal advice prior to those decisions. Because the Board of Supervisors has already addressed those decisions, your request for formal written advice relates to past conduct. Therefore, we decline to provide formal written advice at this time pursuant to Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide formal written advice to Mendocino County Counsel regarding conflict of interest provisions for County Supervisor Dan Hamburg because the request involves analysis of past governmental decisions already made and previously informally advised upon.
